new_classes.definition_en: A material entity used to flavor wine of fresh grapes in the production of vermouth and other aromatized wines under CN heading 2205, including plants, aromatic substances, egg yolks, almonds, and similar additives.

Wine of fresh grapes that has been flavoured with plants or aromatic substances, including vermouth and speciality products such as Marsala all'uovo, Marsala alla mandorla, and Crema di Marsala all'uovo, as covered by CN heading 2205.
A process by which wine of fresh grapes is flavoured with plants or aromatic substances to produce aromatized wine under CN heading 2205.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three core distinguishing criteria of heading 2205: (1) the wine-of-fresh-grapes base (modelled via subClassOf WineOfFreshGrapes), (2) the requirement of flavouring with plants or aromatic substances (modelled via isFlavouredWith some FlavoringAgent), and (3) the aromatization production process. The specific example products (Marsala all'uovo, Marsala alla mandorla, Crema di Marsala all'uovo) and their egg-yolk flavouring ingredient are covered conceptually by FlavoringAgent but not individually enumerated as named individuals or sub-classes; full enumeration of each named product variant would require additional named classes or individuals not warranted at heading level. No quantitative thresholds (e.g. alcohol content limits for aromatized wines) appear in the provided legal note fragment, so none can be axiomatised. The disjointness restriction entry is a modelling imprecision — it was intended to express that AromatizedWine2205 is disjoint from plain WineOfFreshGrapes (non-aromatized), but the complement restriction as placed does not cleanly express this; a proper owl:disjointWith between AromatizedWine2205 and WineOfFreshGrapes parent siblings would be more appropriate but cannot be expressed precisely in the current restriction schema without risking inconsistency. Coverage is therefore rated at 0.72.
